properties.name: Hunga Tonga-Hunga Ha'apai Eruption
properties.description: Massive submarine eruption that generated a tsunami and atmospheric shockwave circling the globe multiple times. One of the most powerful eruptions recorded by modern instruments.

properties.name: Cumbre Vieja Eruption
properties.description: Eruption on La Palma island lasting 85 days, destroying over 2,900 buildings and displacing thousands. Lava flows reached the sea.

properties.name: Kilauea Lower East Rift Zone Eruption
properties.description: Major eruption with fissures opening in Leilani Estates subdivision. Lava destroyed over 700 homes and reshaped the coastline.

properties.name: Volcán de Fuego Eruption
properties.description: Devastating pyroclastic flows buried the village of San Miguel Los Lotes. Deadliest eruption in Guatemala in over a century.

properties.name: Mount Ontake Eruption
properties.description: Sudden phreatic eruption caught hikers by surprise on a popular autumn day. Deadliest volcanic disaster in Japan since 1926.

properties.name: Puyehue-Cordón Caulle Eruption
properties.description: Eruption sent ash plumes across South America and disrupted air travel in the Southern Hemisphere for months.

properties.name: Eyjafjallajökull Eruption
properties.description: Eruption produced enormous ash cloud that shut down European airspace for weeks, stranding millions of passengers worldwide.

properties.name: Mount Merapi Eruption
properties.description: Eruption with pyroclastic flows and lahars prompted evacuations of thousands near Yogyakarta. Part of ongoing Merapi activity cycle.

properties.name: Mount Nyiragongo Eruption
properties.description: Fast-moving lava flows from flank fissures inundated parts of Goma city, displacing 400,000 people and destroying 13% of the city.

properties.name: Hekla Eruption
properties.description: Brief but intense eruption producing a 15 km ash plume and lava flows. Aircraft were diverted from the area.

properties.name: Soufrière Hills Eruption (1997 Peak)
properties.description: Pyroclastic flows on June 25 killed 19 people and destroyed Plymouth, the capital. Two-thirds of the island was declared an exclusion zone.

properties.name: Soufrière Hills Eruption Onset
properties.description: Start of the long-duration eruption that would eventually bury Plymouth. Initial phreatic explosions led to evacuation of southern Montserrat.

properties.name: Mayon Volcano Eruption
properties.description: Pyroclastic flows killed 77 people, many of whom were farmers who had returned to the danger zone prematurely.

properties.name: Mount Pinatubo Eruption
properties.description: Second-largest eruption of the 20th century. Ejected 10 cubic km of material, lowered global temperatures by 0.5°C for two years.

properties.name: Nevado del Ruiz Eruption
properties.description: Eruption melted glacial ice creating massive lahars that buried the town of Armero. One of the deadliest volcanic disasters in history.

properties.name: El Chichón Eruption
properties.description: Three explosive eruptions destroyed the summit and several villages. Aerosol cloud lowered global temperatures by about 0.3°C.

properties.name: Mount St. Helens Eruption
properties.description: Catastrophic lateral blast removed the north face of the mountain. Largest landslide in recorded history accompanied the eruption.

properties.name: Sulawesi Earthquake and Tsunami
properties.description: M7.5 earthquake triggered a devastating tsunami in Palu Bay. Liquefaction destroyed entire neighborhoods.

properties.name: Tōhoku Earthquake and Tsunami
properties.description: M9.1 megathrust earthquake generated a massive tsunami devastating the northeast coast of Japan and triggering the Fukushima nuclear disaster.

properties.name: Chile Earthquake and Tsunami
properties.severity: Wave height up to 29 m locally
properties.description: M8.8 earthquake off Maule generated a Pacific-wide tsunami. Coastal towns in Chile were severely damaged.

properties.name: Samoa Earthquake and Tsunami
properties.description: M8.1 earthquake generated a tsunami that struck Samoa and American Samoa within minutes, devastating coastal villages.

properties.name: Java Earthquake and Tsunami
properties.description: M7.7 earthquake south of Java generated a tsunami that struck the southern coast, devastating the Pangandaran area.

properties.name: Indian Ocean Earthquake and Tsunami
properties.country: Indonesia, Sri Lanka, India, Thailand, Somalia, Maldives
properties.description: M9.1 megathrust earthquake off Sumatra generated the deadliest tsunami in recorded history, affecting 14 countries across the Indian Ocean.

properties.name: Papua New Guinea Tsunami
properties.description: M7.0 earthquake triggered an underwater landslide generating a devastating local tsunami that destroyed Sissano Lagoon villages.

properties.name: Okushiri Island Tsunami
properties.description: M7.8 earthquake in the Sea of Japan generated a tsunami that struck Okushiri Island within minutes, devastating the small fishing town of Aonae.

properties.name: Nicaragua Tsunami
properties.description: Slow 'tsunami earthquake' (M7.7) generated disproportionately large waves along the Nicaraguan coast despite weak ground shaking.

properties.name: Sunda Strait Tsunami (Anak Krakatau)
properties.description: Flank collapse of Anak Krakatau volcano during eruption generated a tsunami without seismic warning, striking western Java and southern Sumatra.

properties.name: Greenfield Iowa Tornado
properties.description: Violent tornado struck the small town of Greenfield, Iowa, destroying much of the downtown area and killing five residents.

properties.name: Rolling Fork Mississippi Tornado
properties.description: Powerful nighttime tornado devastated the town of Rolling Fork, Mississippi, leveling entire blocks.

properties.name: December 2021 Kentucky Tornado Outbreak
properties.description: Long-track supercell produced a tornado traveling over 165 miles, devastating Mayfield, KY and other communities across multiple states.

properties.name: Mayfield Kentucky Tornado
properties.description: Tornado destroyed the Mayfield Consumer Products candle factory and much of downtown Mayfield during December outbreak.

properties.name: Dayton Ohio Tornado Outbreak
properties.description: Powerful late-night tornado tore through Dayton suburbs as part of a broader outbreak across the Miami Valley.

properties.name: Kokomo Indiana Tornado
properties.description: Series of tornadoes struck central Indiana, with the strongest hitting Kokomo and causing significant damage to homes and a Starbucks.

properties.name: 2013 Moore Oklahoma Tornado
properties.description: Violent EF5 tornado carved a 17-mile path through Moore, destroying two elementary schools and thousands of structures. Winds estimated at 210 mph.

properties.name: 2013 El Reno Oklahoma Tornado
properties.description: Widest tornado ever recorded at 2.6 miles wide with 295 mph subvortex winds. Killed storm chasers Tim Samaras and his team.

properties.name: Joplin Missouri Tornado
properties.description: Deadliest US tornado since 1947. EF5 tornado carved a mile-wide path through Joplin, destroying St. John's hospital and thousands of buildings.

properties.name: 2011 Super Outbreak (April 27)
properties.description: Largest tornado outbreak in history with 362 confirmed tornadoes in a single 24-hour period, including four EF5 tornadoes across the Southeast.

properties.name: Tuscaloosa-Birmingham Tornado
properties.description: Massive tornado cut a mile-wide path through Tuscaloosa and Birmingham during the April 27 Super Outbreak.

properties.name: Super Tuesday Tornado Outbreak
properties.description: Major outbreak on February 5-6 produced 87 tornadoes across the Southeast, with the deadliest tornadoes striking Tennessee.

properties.name: Greensburg Kansas Tornado
properties.description: EF5 tornado with 205 mph winds destroyed 95% of the town of Greensburg. The town later rebuilt as a model green community.

properties.name: Bridge Creek-Moore Tornado
properties.description: Produced the highest wind speed ever recorded on Earth at 302 mph (DOW radar). Destroyed 8,000 homes in the Oklahoma City metro area.

properties.name: 1999 Oklahoma City Tornado Outbreak
properties.description: Outbreak produced 66 tornadoes on May 3, including the Bridge Creek-Moore F5 and several other violent tornadoes across central Oklahoma and Kansas.

properties.name: 2024 South Asia Heatwave
properties.severity: Temperatures exceeding 50°C in parts of India and Pakistan
properties.description: Extreme heatwave with temperatures above 50°C in Rajasthan and Sindh. Widespread heat-related deaths and water shortages across the subcontinent.

properties.name: 2023 Southern European Heatwave (Cerberus/Charon)
properties.description: Back-to-back heatwaves named Cerberus and Charon broke temperature records across southern Europe. Greece experienced devastating wildfires.

properties.name: 2022 European Heatwave
properties.severity: UK record 40.3°C; France, Spain, Portugal exceeded 45°C
properties.description: Record-breaking heat across Western Europe. UK exceeded 40°C for the first time. Over 60,000 heat-related deaths estimated across the continent.

properties.name: 2021 Pacific Northwest Heat Dome
properties.description: Unprecedented heat dome shattered all-time records. Lytton, BC set Canada's all-time record at 49.6°C before being destroyed by wildfire the next day.

properties.name: 2020 Siberian Heatwave
properties.description: Prolonged warmth across Siberia reached 38°C in Verkhoyansk, the highest temperature ever recorded above the Arctic Circle. Triggered massive wildfires and permafrost thaw.

properties.name: 2019 European Heatwaves (June/July)
properties.severity: France record 46°C; records broken in Germany, Belgium, Netherlands
properties.description: Two successive heatwaves in June and July broke national all-time records across Western Europe. France hit 46°C for the first time.

properties.name: 2018 Japan Heatwave
properties.description: Deadly heatwave following historic floods. Kumagaya set Japan's all-time record at 41.1°C. Over 1,000 deaths and 50,000+ hospitalizations.

properties.name: 2017 Lucifer Heatwave
properties.severity: Temperatures up to 46°C in southern Italy
properties.description: Heatwave nicknamed 'Lucifer' brought extreme temperatures across southern Europe, fueling wildfires in Italy, Croatia, and Greece.

properties.name: 2015 India-Pakistan Heatwave
properties.description: Devastating heatwave killed over 2,500 in India and 1,600 in Pakistan. Karachi alone suffered over 1,200 deaths in a single week.

properties.name: 2013 Australian Heatwave (Angry Summer)
properties.severity: National average record 40.3°C; new color added to weather maps
properties.description: Extreme heat so intense that the Bureau of Meteorology added new colors to weather maps. National average temperature set a record at 40.3°C.

properties.name: 2012 North American Heatwave
properties.severity: Hottest year on record for contiguous US at the time
properties.description: Record-breaking heat across the central and eastern US. 2012 became the hottest year on record for the contiguous United States. Severe drought accompanied the heat.

properties.name: 2010 Russian Heatwave
properties.description: Worst heatwave in Russian history. Moscow recorded 38.2°C as peat and forest fires blanketed the city in toxic smog. Estimated 55,000 excess deaths.

properties.name: 2006 European Heatwave
properties.severity: UK record 36.5°C; widespread over 40°C on continent
properties.description: July heatwave brought scorching temperatures across Western Europe, with hundreds of excess deaths in France, the Netherlands and the UK.

properties.name: 2003 European Heatwave
properties.country: France, Italy, Spain, Portugal, Germany, UK
properties.severity: France exceeded 44°C; hottest European summer in 500 years
properties.description: Deadliest European natural disaster in decades. France alone lost nearly 15,000 people. Hottest European summer in at least 500 years according to paleoclimate data.

properties.name: Winter Storm Uri (Cold Wave)
properties.severity: Temperatures -19°C in Dallas; coldest in Texas since 1899
properties.description: Historic Arctic outbreak brought record cold to Texas and the Deep South, collapsing the power grid and leaving millions without heat for days.

properties.name: 2019 Polar Vortex Cold Wave
properties.severity: Wind chill -56°C in Minnesota; Chicago -30°C actual
properties.description: Polar vortex displacement brought the coldest temperatures in a generation to the Upper Midwest. Chicago recorded -30°C, colder than parts of Antarctica.

properties.name: Beast from the East
properties.country: United Kingdom, Ireland, France, Netherlands, Germany
properties.severity: UK temperatures below -10°C; heavy snowfall across Europe
properties.description: Sudden stratospheric warming event displaced polar vortex, driving Siberian air across Europe. Heavy snow paralyzed the UK and Ireland.

properties.name: 2014 North American Polar Vortex
properties.severity: Wind chills below -50°C; record lows across Midwest and East
properties.description: Polar vortex disruption brought Arctic air deep into the Eastern US in early January. All 50 states experienced below-freezing temperatures.

properties.name: 2012 European Cold Wave
properties.country: Ukraine, Poland, Romania, Russia, Italy, France
properties.description: Severe cold wave in late January and February killed hundreds across Europe, particularly homeless populations in Eastern European countries.

properties.name: 2010 European Cold Wave
properties.severity: UK temperatures below -20°C; heaviest December snow in decades
properties.description: Extreme cold and heavy snowfall in late November through December paralyzed transportation across Europe. UK experienced coldest December in 100 years.

properties.name: 1985 United States Cold Wave
properties.severity: Record lows below -40°C; Florida citrus devastated
properties.description: Extreme Arctic outbreak in January sent temperatures plunging across the Eastern US. Florida's citrus industry suffered over $1 billion in damage.

properties.name: 1983 United States Cold Wave
properties.severity: Record lows; wind chills below -60°C in Midwest
properties.description: December 1983 cold wave brought record-breaking cold to much of the US. Over 270 deaths attributed to the extreme temperatures.

properties.name: 2022 Buffalo Blizzard (Elliott)
properties.severity: 125 cm snow; winds 110 km/h; whiteout conditions for 36+ hours
properties.description: Historic Christmas blizzard trapped residents in vehicles and homes for days. Lake-effect snow combined with an Arctic cyclone to paralyze Buffalo.

properties.name: Blizzard Jonas (Snowzilla)
properties.severity: Up to 107 cm snow; hurricane-force gusts on coast
properties.description: Massive nor'easter buried the Mid-Atlantic and Northeast. New York City received its second-largest snowfall on record. Widespread coastal flooding.

properties.name: Blizzard Juno
properties.severity: Up to 91 cm snow; blizzard warnings for 29 million people
properties.description: Powerful nor'easter brought heavy snow and strong winds to New England. Travel bans implemented in New York and New England states.

properties.name: 2014 Atlanta Ice Storm / Southern Blizzard
properties.severity: 5-8 cm ice/snow paralyzing Southeast; 10,000+ vehicles stranded
properties.description: Light snow and ice brought the Atlanta metropolitan area to a standstill. Thousands of motorists were stranded on frozen highways for over 24 hours.

properties.name: Snowmageddon (February 2010)
properties.severity: Up to 100 cm snow; back-to-back storms
properties.description: Two major blizzards struck the Mid-Atlantic within a week, burying Washington DC under record snowfall. Federal government closed for a full week.

properties.name: 2008 Afghanistan Blizzard
properties.severity: Temperatures below -30°C; extreme snowfall in Hindu Kush
properties.description: Extreme cold wave and blizzards killed nearly 1,000 people across Afghanistan. Livestock losses devastating to rural communities.

properties.name: 1996 North American Blizzard
properties.severity: Up to 120 cm snow; record snowfall in multiple cities
properties.description: Massive blizzard in January paralyzed the Eastern Seaboard from Virginia to New England. Followed by severe flooding during rapid thaw.

properties.name: 1993 Storm of the Century
properties.severity: Up to 140 cm snow; hurricane-force winds; record low pressures
properties.description: Superstorm combined characteristics of a blizzard, hurricane, and tornado outbreak. Affected the entire Eastern Seaboard from Cuba to Canada.

properties.name: 1978 Northeastern United States Blizzard
properties.severity: Up to 69 cm snow; winds 175 km/h; 10-foot storm surge
properties.description: Devastating nor'easter paralyzed New England for over a week. Rhode Island declared a state of emergency as coastal flooding and snow buried communities.

properties.name: Winter Storm Uri Ice Storm
properties.severity: Up to 2.5 cm ice accumulation; mass power grid failure
properties.description: Ice storm component of Winter Storm Uri caused catastrophic ice accumulation across Texas, collapsing power infrastructure and leaving 4.5 million without electricity.

properties.name: 2023 Tennessee/Southeast Ice Storm
properties.description: Significant ice storm across Tennessee and the Southeast caused widespread power outages and hazardous travel. Over 100,000 customers lost power.

properties.name: 2013 Central US Ice Storm
properties.description: Major ice storm across Kansas, Oklahoma, and Missouri in December. Downed trees and power lines left hundreds of thousands without electricity.

properties.name: 2009 Kentucky/Arkansas Ice Storm
properties.severity: Up to 5 cm ice accumulation; worst in Kentucky history
properties.description: Catastrophic ice storm left 1.3 million without power across Kentucky and Arkansas. Some areas went without electricity for weeks in frigid conditions.

properties.name: 1998 North American Ice Storm
properties.severity: Up to 10 cm ice accumulation; worst in Canadian history
properties.description: Most destructive ice storm in Canadian history. Over 4 million lost power in Quebec and Ontario. Electrical pylons collapsed under massive ice loads. 35 deaths across the region.

properties.name: 1995 Chicago Heatwave
properties.severity: Heat index up to 54°C; temperatures over 41°C
properties.description: Five-day heatwave killed over 700 people in Chicago alone. Disproportionately affected elderly and low-income residents without air conditioning.
